nodes.name: Extract Invoice Details as JSON

parameters.text:
=Analyze this markdown content and convert it to JSON following this exact schema:
{
    "invoice_details": {
        "statement_date": "",
        "organization": {
            "name": "",
            "address": "",
            "gst_number": ""
        },
        "member": {
            "name": "",
            "company": "",
            "address": "",
            "contact_number": "",
            "organization_number": ""
        }
    },
    "transactions": [
        {
            "date": "",
            "document_number": "",
            "description": "",
            "base_amount": 0.00,
            "gst": 0.00,
            "bc_pst": 0.00,
            "bc_pst_liquor": 0.00,  # Added for liquor PST
            "container_deposit": 0.00,  # Added for bottle deposits
            "service_fee": 0.00,
            "tip_amount": 0.00,
            "regular_price": 0.00,  # Added for regular price tracking
            "savings_amount": 0.00,  # Added for savings tracking
            "total_charge": 0.00,
            "transaction_type": ""
        }
    ],
    "payment_details": {
        "previous_balance": 0.00,
        "payment_amount": 0.00,
        "payment_reference": "",
        "payment_date": "",
        "payment_method": "",
        "payment_status": "",
        "card_number": "",  # Added for card details
        "auth_number": "",  # Added for authorization
        "transaction_number": ""
    },
    "invoice_summary": {
        "subtotal": 0.00,
        "service_fees_total": 0.00,
        "tips_total": 0.00,
        "current_excl_gst": 0.00,
        "container_deposit_total": 0.00,  # Added for deposits
        "outstanding_gst": 0.00,
        "paid_gst": 0.00,
        "total_gst": 0.00,
        "total_bc_pst": 0.00,
        "total_bc_pst_liquor": 0.00,  # Added for liquor PST if shown in markdown content
        "total_savings": 0.00,  # Added for savings
        "final_amount_due": 0.00
    },
    "payment_terms": {
        "due_date": "",
        "processing_date": "",
        "special_notices": [],
        "cancellation_policy": "",
        "refund_policy": "",
        "return_policy": ""  # Added for return policy
    },
    "additional_info": {
        "booking_number": "",
        "transaction_time": "",  # Added for transaction time
        "register_info": "",     # Added for register details
        "event_details": {
            "date": "",
            "time": "",
            "location": ""
        },
        "special_instructions": []
    },
    "summary": "" # The natural language summary of the invoice
}

Markdown Content:
{{ $json.data }}

Important:
- Extract exact values from the markdown
- Return only valid JSON
- Include all fields even if empty
- Format numbers as floats with 2 decimal places
- Track container deposits separately
- Show liquor PST (10%) separately from regular PST if provided in markdown content
- Include regular prices and savings amounts
- Track transaction details including card info and authorization
- Parse return policy information
- Include register and transaction time details
- Ensure final_amount_due equals the sum of all applicable charges and taxes
- Summarize the markdown contents
- Only output valid JSON without any preamble or further explanation.  Remove any ```json and ``` from response.

nodes.name: Summarize Document

parameters.text:
=Please analyze this document and provide:

## Document Analysis
- A concise executive summary (2-3 sentences)
- Key themes and main points
- Notable findings or insights
- Data highlights and important statistics (if applicable)

## Recommendations
- Action items or next steps
- Areas requiring further investigation
- Potential implications

## Format Requirements
- Structure the analysis using clear headers and sections
- Include relevant quotes to support key points
- Present any numerical data in tables or bullet points
- Highlight critical information using bold text

Please maintain the original document's context while making the content more accessible and actionable.

Here is the document: {{ $json.data }}

nodes.name: Sticky Note9

parameters.content:
# Description

This workflow automates document processing using LlamaParse to extract and analyze text from various file formats. It intelligently processes documents, extracts structured data, and delivers actionable insights through multiple channels.

## How It Works

### Document Ingestion & Processing 📄
- Monitors Gmail for incoming attachments or accepts documents via webhook
- Validates file formats against supported LlamaParse extensions
- Uploads documents to LlamaParse for advanced text extraction
- Stores original documents in Google Drive for reference

### Intelligent Document Analysis 🧠
- Automatically classifies document types (invoices, reports, etc.)
- Extracts structured data using customized AI prompts
- Generates comprehensive document summaries with key insights
- Converts unstructured text into organized JSON data

### Invoice Processing Automation 💼
- Extracts critical invoice details (dates, amounts, line items)
- Organizes financial data into structured formats
- Calculates tax breakdowns, subtotals, and payment information
- Maintains detailed records for accounting purposes

### Multi-Channel Delivery 📱
- Saves extracted data to Google Sheets for tracking and analysis
- Sends concise summaries via Telegram for immediate review
- Creates searchable document archives in Google Drive
- Updates spreadsheets with structured financial information

## Setup Steps

### Configure API Credentials 🔑
- Set up LlamaParse API connection
- Configure Gmail OAuth for email monitoring
- Set up Google Drive and Sheets integrations
- Add Telegram bot credentials for notifications

### Customize AI Processing ⚙️
- Adjust document classification parameters
- Modify extraction templates for specific document types
- Fine-tune summary generation prompts
- Customize invoice data extraction schema

### Test and Deploy 🚀
- Test with sample documents of various formats
- Verify data extraction accuracy
- Confirm notification delivery
- Monitor processing pipeline performance

# ✨🔪 Advanced AI Powered Document Parsing & Text Extraction with Llama Parse

# Description

This workflow automates document processing using LlamaParse to extract and analyze text from various file formats. It intelligently processes documents, extracts structured data, and delivers actionable insights through multiple channels.

## How It Works

### Document Ingestion & Processing 📄
- Monitors Gmail for incoming attachments or accepts documents via webhook
- Validates file formats against supported LlamaParse extensions
- Uploads documents to LlamaParse for advanced text extraction
- Stores original documents in Google Drive for reference

### Intelligent Document Analysis 🧠
- Automatically classifies document types (invoices, reports, etc.)
- Extracts structured data using customized AI prompts
- Generates comprehensive document summaries with key insights
- Converts unstructured text into organized JSON data

### Invoice Processing Automation 💼
- Extracts critical invoice details (dates, amounts, line items)
- Organizes financial data into structured formats
- Calculates tax breakdowns, subtotals, and payment information
- Maintains detailed records for accounting purposes

### Multi-Channel Delivery 📱
- Saves extracted data to Google Sheets for tracking and analysis
- Sends concise summaries via Telegram for immediate review
- Creates searchable document archives in Google Drive
- Updates spreadsheets with structured financial information

## Setup Steps

### Configure API Credentials 🔑
- Set up LlamaParse API connection
- Configure Gmail OAuth for email monitoring
- Set up Google Drive and Sheets integrations
- Add Telegram bot credentials for notifications

### Customize AI Processing ⚙️
- Adjust document classification parameters
- Modify extraction templates for specific document types
- Fine-tune summary generation prompts
- Customize invoice data extraction schema

### Test and Deploy 🚀
- Test with sample documents of various formats
- Verify data extraction accuracy
- Confirm notification delivery
- Monitor processing pipeline performance

nodes.name: Update Google Sheet by LlamaParse ID

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
google Drive  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Locate the webhook node named "Webhook".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Verify the settings of the trigger node "Gmail Trigger".
Open the "Gmail" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Limit" node (n8n-nodes-base.limit) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Message" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Parse Document with LlamaParse" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Summarize Email" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "gpt-4o-mini" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Save LlamaParse ID and Summary to Google Sheets" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Save Document to Google Drive" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleDrive) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Extract Invoice Details as JSON" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Incoming triggers will qualified/formatted, new rows will be written to Google Sheets, and email replies or alerts will be sent automatically.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
